69f6ccbea8d8a47437e959b5.question: A character in this opera asks, “Am I in your light?” in one aria before her partner responds by quoting a poem by Baudelaire. The opening line of a refrain in this opera changes whether it refers to north, west, or east each time it appears. This opera’s composer was inspired by Sibelius’s Seventh Symphony to create a symphony based on it whose second movement is titled “Panic.” A 2018 production of this opera with Ryan McKinny in the lead role featured indigenous artists performing a Corn Dance. In this opera, the maid Pasqualita describes the blossoming of a “cloud-flower” while singing a lullaby adapted from a traditional Tewa melody. Act 1 of this opera ends with an aria based on Donne’s “Batter My Heart, Three-Person’d God.” For 10 points, name this opera by John Adams about J. Robert Oppenheimer.
69f6ccbea8d8a47437e959b5.question_sanitized: A character in this opera asks, "Am I in your light?" in one aria before her partner responds by quoting a poem by Baudelaire. The opening line of a refrain in this opera changes whether it refers to north, west, or east each time it appears. This opera's composer was inspired by Sibelius's Seventh Symphony to create a symphony based on it whose second movement is titled "Panic." A 2018 production of this opera with Ryan McKinny in the lead role featured indigenous artists performing a Corn Dance. In this opera, the maid Pasqualita describes the blossoming of a "cloud-flower" while singing a lullaby adapted from a traditional Tewa melody. Act 1 of this opera ends with an aria based on Donne's "Batter My Heart, Three-Person'd God." For 10 points, name this opera by John Adams about J. Robert Oppenheimer.

69f6ccbfa8d8a47437e95a41.leadin: This opera’s most commonly excerpted selections include the “Fête polonaise” and the “Danse slave.” For 10 points each:
69f6ccbfa8d8a47437e95a41.leadin_sanitized: This opera's most commonly excerpted selections include the "Fete polonaise" and the "Danse slave." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1887 opera about Henri de Valois’s decision to rule France even after being elected leader by Polish nobles. A student of this opera’s composer claimed that its prelude “changed the course of French harmony.”

This composer uttered that quote about Chabrier’s Le roi malgré lui. His own operas include L’enfant et les sortilèges (“lawn-FAWN ay lay sor-tee-LEZH”) and one about Concepción, the unfaithful wife of a Spanish clockmaker.

Le roi malgré lui received its US premiere at this prestigious performing arts conservatory in New York City. At one point, over half of the New York Philharmonic trained at this conservatory’s school of music, whose campus is in Lincoln Center.
parts_sanitized: Name this 1887 opera about Henri de Valois's decision to rule France even after being elected leader by Polish nobles. A student of this opera's composer claimed that its prelude "changed the course of French harmony."

This composer uttered that quote about Chabrier's Le roi malgre lui. His own operas include L'enfant et les sortileges ("lawn-FAWN ay lay sor-tee-LEZH") and one about Concepcion, the unfaithful wife of a Spanish clockmaker.

Le roi malgre lui received its US premiere at this prestigious performing arts conservatory in New York City. At one point, over half of the New York Philharmonic trained at this conservatory's school of music, whose campus is in Lincoln Center.
answers: Le roi malgré lui(“luh WAH mahl-GRAY l’wee”) [or The King in Spite of Himself; or The King Despite Himself; or The Reluctant King] (by Emmanuel Chabrier)
Maurice Ravel [or Joseph Maurice Ravel] (The other opera is L’heure espagnole.)
answers_sanitized: Le roi malgre lui("luh WAH mahl-GRAY l'wee") [or The King in Spite of Himself; or The King Despite Himself; or The Reluctant King] (by Emmanuel Chabrier)
Maurice Ravel [or Joseph Maurice Ravel] (The other opera is L'heure espagnole.)

69f6ccc1a8d8a47437e95b19.leadin: Some of the major operas premiered by this company include Debussy’s Pelléas et Mélisande and Offenbach’s The Tales of Hoffmann. For 10 points each:
69f6ccc1a8d8a47437e95b19.leadin_sanitized: Some of the major operas premiered by this company include Debussy's Pelleas et Melisande and Offenbach's The Tales of Hoffmann. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Parisian opera company housed at the Salle Favart. It shares its name with a genre of French opera that uses spoken dialogue rather than recitatives (“reh-sit-uh-TEEVS”) and that can end happily or unhappily, despite their name.

The Opéra-Comique premiered three of this composer’s operas: Esclarmonde, Werther, and Manon. It did not, however, premiere this composer’s opera Thaïs (“tye-EESS”), which includes a Méditation for violin and orchestra.
In 1883, the Opéra-Comique premiered this opera, whose title character sings the solo “Bell Song” and the duet “Dôme épais le jasmin” (“dome ay-PAY luh zhazz-MAN”) with her servant Mallika.
parts_sanitized: Name this Parisian opera company housed at the Salle Favart. It shares its name with a genre of French opera that uses spoken dialogue rather than recitatives ("reh-sit-uh-TEEVS") and that can end happily or unhappily, despite their name.

The Opera-Comique premiered three of this composer's operas: Esclarmonde, Werther, and Manon. It did not, however, premiere this composer's opera Thais ("tye-EESS"), which includes a Meditation for violin and orchestra.
In 1883, the Opera-Comique premiered this opera, whose title character sings the solo "Bell Song" and the duet "Dome epais le jasmin" ("dome ay-PAY luh zhazz-MAN") with her servant Mallika.
answers_sanitized: Opera-Comique [or Theatre national de l'Opera-Comique; reject "comic opera"]
Jules Massenet [or Jules Emile Frederic Massenet]

69f6ccc3a8d8a47437e95c46.question: The vocal part in a soprano aria set in this country ends by descending from E-flat to F over the chords F minor and B-flat minor on the word “aita.” An aria sung by a character in this country ends by calling the singer’s beloved “mia vita mio solo fior” or “only flower of my life.” The aria “Sola, perduta, abbandonata” (“SOH-luh pair-DOO-tuh ah-bahn-doh-NAH-tuh”) has been mocked for falsely portraying a real-life city in this country as being on the outskirts of a desert. In another opera, two characters from this country conclude the aria “Dovunque al mondo” (“doh-VOON-kway al MON-doh”) by singing “[this country] for ever!” over an excerpt from this country’s national anthem. A man in this country sings “Ch’ella mi creda libero” (“KELL-uh mee CRAY-duh LEE-bair-oh”) as he is about to be hanged for being the bandit Ramerrez. For 10 points, a Puccini opera titled for a fanciulla (“fahn-CHOO-luh”)is set in what country, where Pinkerton hails from in Madama Butterfly?
69f6ccc3a8d8a47437e95c46.question_sanitized: The vocal part in a soprano aria set in this country ends by descending from E-flat to F over the chords F minor and B-flat minor on the word "aita." An aria sung by a character in this country ends by calling the singer's beloved "mia vita mio solo fior" or "only flower of my life." The aria "Sola, perduta, abbandonata" ("SOH-luh pair-DOO-tuh ah-bahn-doh-NAH-tuh") has been mocked for falsely portraying a real-life city in this country as being on the outskirts of a desert. In another opera, two characters from this country conclude the aria "Dovunque al mondo" ("doh-VOON-kway al MON-doh") by singing "[this country] for ever!" over an excerpt from this country's national anthem. A man in this country sings "Ch'ella mi creda libero" ("KELL-uh mee CRAY-duh LEE-bair-oh") as he is about to be hanged for being the bandit Ramerrez. For 10 points, a Puccini opera titled for a fanciulla ("fahn-CHOO-luh")is set in what country, where Pinkerton hails from in Madama Butterfly?
69f6ccc3a8d8a47437e95c46.answer_sanitized: United States [or USA or United States of America]
